name: Apple Pancake Puff
recipeIngredient: 3 small apples, cored and cut into 1/4 inch thick slices
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy medium ovenproof skillet, melt 2 teaspoons of the margarine.
2. Add apples, raisins, 1 tablespoon sugar, orange juice, and cinnamon to the skillet. Cook, stirring frequently for 6-8 minutes, until apples are just tender. Remove apples to a bowl.
3. Preheat the oven to 425°F (220°C).
4. In a small bowl with an electric mixer, beat eggs until foamy. Gradually add milk, flour, remaining 2 tablespoons sugar, and vanilla; mix for 2 minutes longer, until batter is smooth.
5. Add the remaining 2 teaspoons margarine to the skillet. Place in the oven to melt for about 1 minute.
6. Pour batter into the skillet. Bake for 10 minutes.
7. Remove from the oven and spoon apple mixture into the center of the pancake. Return to the oven and bake for 12-15 minutes longer, until puffed and golden.
8. Cut into quarters. Each serving contains: 1/2 fat, 1/2 protein, 1/2 carbohydrates, 1 fiber, 60 calories.
